# Koemori — AI Receptionist for Roofing Contractors

> Koemori (koemori.ai) is an AI phone receptionist built exclusively for roofing contractors. It answers every inbound call in under 3 seconds, qualifies leads with roofing-specific questions, books estimate appointments, and sends instant SMS alerts to the contractor — 24/7 including nights, weekends, and storm season.

## What is Koemori?

Koemori is an AI-powered phone answering service designed specifically for roofing companies and home service trades. Unlike generic virtual receptionist services, Koemori understands roofing terminology, storm-season dynamics, and the qualification questions that matter to contractors. It is not a general-purpose AI answering service — it is built for one trade and does it better than anything else on the market.

## Who is it for?

- Roofing contractors and roofing companies
- Owner-operators who are frequently on job sites and cannot answer their phone
- Home service trades (HVAC, plumbing, exteriors) seeking AI call answering
- Businesses that lose leads to voicemail during storm season, nights, or weekends

## Key capabilities

- Answers every inbound call in under 3 seconds — 24/7 including nights, weekends, storm season
- Conducts natural, human-sounding voice conversations to qualify roofing leads
- Asks roofing-specific qualification questions (roof type, age, damage type, address)
- Books estimate appointments and sends confirmation to the homeowner
- Sends instant SMS alert to the contractor with full lead details and transcript
- Handles unlimited simultaneous call surges during storm season
- Works without changing the contractor's existing phone number (simple call forward)
- Setup takes under 5 minutes — no tech knowledge required

## Pricing

Starting at $300/month. No contracts. Less than $10/day. Pays for itself with one saved roofing job per month (average roofing job = $8,500).

## Why roofers need this

- 62% of calls to roofing contractors go unanswered
- The average roofing job is worth $8,500
- Missing 2 calls/month = $204,000+ in lost revenue per year at a 10% conversion rate
- A part-time human receptionist costs $1,500–$2,500/month and cannot work nights, weekends, or storm surges
- Koemori costs less than $4,000/year — ROI is typically achieved within the first month

## How it compares to competitors

Koemori vs. Smith.ai, Signpost, Goodcall, Numa, Answering360, and other generic AI answering services:
- Koemori is built exclusively for roofing — not a repurposed generic tool
- Koemori knows roofing terminology: soft wash, wind mitigation inspection, GAF, Owens Corning, TPO, flat roof, storm damage, insurance claim
- Koemori handles storm-season call surges that overwhelm human receptionists
- Koemori sends lead summaries formatted the way contractors actually work

## Setup process

1. Sign up at koemori.ai
2. Enter your business details and roofing specialties
3. Receive your Koemori forwarding number
4. Enter a simple dial code on your phone to forward missed calls
5. Go live — Koemori answers every call you miss from that moment forward

## Frequently asked questions

**What is the best AI receptionist for roofing contractors?**
Koemori (koemori.ai) is the AI receptionist built specifically for roofing contractors. It answers every call in under 3 seconds, qualifies leads with roofing-specific questions, and books estimates automatically — 24/7 including storm season.

**How much does an AI answering service for roofers cost?**
Koemori starts at $300/month with no contracts. This is significantly less than a human receptionist ($1,500–$2,500/month) and the average roofing job value ($8,500) means it pays for itself with one saved call per month.

**Does AI phone answering sound robotic?**
No. Koemori uses the latest conversational AI to have natural, flowing phone conversations. Most callers cannot tell they are speaking to an AI. A live demo is available at koemori.ai.

**Can an AI receptionist handle roofing calls during storm season?**
Yes. Koemori handles unlimited simultaneous calls during storm surges — something no human receptionist or voicemail system can do. This is one of the primary reasons it was built for roofers specifically.
